name: Fort Campbell
tagline: Home of the 101st Airborne Division (Air Assault), 5th Special Forces Group, and 160th SOAR.
_note: Killer-content (two-state decision) is drafted/real. Other sections populated from authoritative sources with per-section source + verified date; volatile data (gate hours) links to the official page instead of stating values. Active-duty population left out (not found on an official fact sheet).

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Fort Campbell straddles the Kentucky-Tennessee border and is one of the Army's premier power-projection platforms, home to the 101st Airborne Division (Air Assault).
It also hosts significant special-operations forces, including the 5th Special Forces Group and the 160th Special Operations Aviation Regiment (SOAR).

sections.title: Tennessee or Kentucky? The Two-State Decision

data.subtitle: For buyers and renters PCSing to Campbell, the side of the line you choose is a several-thousand-dollar decision over a typical tour.
intro: Fort Campbell sits on the Kentucky-Tennessee state line. The post office address is in Kentucky, but most of the housing, most of the gates, and the largest off-post community (Clarksville) sit on the Tennessee side. That geography forces a decision other military families never have to make: which state to live in.

The two states tax differently, school children differently, and value homes differently. Over a typical three-to-four-year tour the cumulative difference between Clarksville and Hopkinsville or Oak Grove adds up to thousands of dollars, plus a meaningfully different schooling and real estate experience.

recommendations.who: Buyers with school-age kids, 3-4 yr tour
recommendations.advice: Default to Clarksville (Sango or Rossview): stronger schools, faster resale, tax math roughly a wash.
Look hard at Hopkinsville. You get noticeably more house per budget, but plan for a slower sale at your next PCS.
Oak Grove. Base proximity is unmatched.
Tennessee (Clarksville) for the schools; the tax advantage compounds less over a short lease.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Fort Campbell has multiple access gates, including Gate 4 (TC Freeman) and Gate 7, the main 24/7 visitor and commercial gate. Gate openings, hours, and lanes change frequently.

Visitors without a DoD ID need a pass from the Visitor Control Center (Gate 7, Building 7199, 7199 Glider Road; 270-956-4488). Bring a REAL ID-compliant ID plus your vehicle registration and insurance.
IMPORTANT: gate hours change often. Always confirm which gates are open and their current hours on the official garrison page before you travel.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: On-post housing is operated by Campbell Crossing LLC, a Lendlease privatized military housing community.

Homes are available to military families, unaccompanied service members, reservists, DoD employees, retirees, and qualified civilians. Active-duty service members pay no application fees or security deposits, and you apply through the partner's online application.
Main office: (931) 431-9003. Confirm current numbers and availability on the partner site.

source.label: DoD BAH Rate Lookup (MHA KY106, CY2026)

sections.title: Schools
data: Families in on-post housing are eligible for DoDEA schools. The Department of Defense Education Activity operates 6 schools at Fort Campbell serving PK through grade 12.

Off post, the Tennessee side is served primarily by the Clarksville-Montgomery County School System (CMCSS); the Kentucky side primarily by Christian County Public Schools. Other nearby districts include Stewart and Cheatham (TN) and Todd and Trigg (KY).

data.Services: Primary care, pediatrics, behavioral health, pharmacy, lab, and radiology
